workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: Just came out of forecast prep with Lucerne Publishing. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-03-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:05:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:13:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:15:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:20:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:30:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:34:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:39:00] David (Lucerne Publishing): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Lucerne Publishing: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF45-P trap (Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking); produces complete Health delta assessment, whitespace hypotheses, renewal risk with confidence, action distribution wi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-P failure mode (Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF45-P -- Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
